name: adagio

metadata.name: adagio
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: The Dag IO Framework for Fugue projects

documentation:
# A-DAG-IO Framework

[![GitHub release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/fugue-project/adagio.svg)](https://GitHub.com/fugue-project/adagio)
[![PyPI pyversions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/adagio.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/adagio/)
[![PyPI license](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/adagio.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/adagio/)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/adagio.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/adagio/)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/fugue-project/adagio/branch/master/graph/badge.svg?token=P82AEINHFY)](https://codecov.io/gh/fugue-project/adagio)

[Join Fugue-Project on Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/fugue-project/shared_invite/zt-he6tcazr-OCkj2GEv~J9UYoZT3FPM4g)

A Dag IO framework for [Fugue projects](https://github.com/fugue-project)

## Installation

```
pip install adagio
```

classes.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowContextMember
documentation:
Base class for components of func:`~adagio.instances.WorkflowContext`

```
param wf_ctx

parent workflow context
```

classes.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowResultCache

methods.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowResultCache.set
documentation:
Set `key` with `value`

```
param key

uuid string

param value

any value
```

methods.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowResultCache.get
documentation:
Try to get value for `key`

```
param key

uuid string

return

<hasvalue>, <skipped>, <value>
```

Interface for cachine workflow task outputs. This cache is
normally for cross execution retrieval.

The implementation should be thread safe, and all methods should catch all
exceptions and not raise.

classes.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowContext

methods.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowContext.abort
documentation: Call this function to abort a running workflow

methods.name: adagio.instances.WorkflowContext.run
documentation:
Instantiate and run a workflow spec

```
param spec

workflow spec

param conf

configs to initialize the workflow
```

Context of the workflow instance

```
param cache

cache type, instance or string representation,
defaults to NoOpCache

param engine

engine type, instance or string representation,
defaults to SequentialExecutionEngine

param hooks

hooks type, instance or string representation,
defaults to WorkflowHooks

param logger

hooks type, instance or string representation,
defaults to None (logging.getLogger())

param config

dict like configurations
```

methods.name: adagio.instances.TaskContext.ensure_all_ready
documentation:
This is a blocking call to wait for all config values
and input dependencies to be set by the upstreams.

methods.name: adagio.instances.TaskContext.configs
documentation:
Dictionary of config values. It's lazy, only when a certain key
is requested, it will block the requesting thread until that
key's value is ready.

methods.name: adagio.instances.TaskContext.inputs
documentation:
Dictionary of input dependencies. It's lazy, only when a certain key
is requested, it will block the requesting thread until that
key's value is ready.

methods.name: adagio.instances.TaskContext.outputs
documentation:
Dictionary of outputs. For outputs in the spec but you don't set:
if the caller finished with no exception the framework will set them
to `skipped`, otherwise, will set them to `failed` with the caller's
exception

methods.name: adagio.instances._Input._cache
documentation:
Get value and cache so following `get` calls will just return
the cached value. This function should not be called by users. It
must be called on a single thread.

```
return

cached value
```

methods.name: adagio.instances._Task._ensure_fully_connected
documentation:
By design, this should be called always when fully connected,
but if this failed, it means there is a bug in the framework itself.
